paper_title: Developing an Intelligent Data Analysis Approach for Marine Sediments

methods.description: This method involves using multivariate statistical techniques such as hierarchical and non-hierarchical clustering, principal components analysis, and Thurston-Spengler apportionment procedure to detect patterns of similarity between variables and/or objects in marine sediment data.

steps.description: Standardization of the raw data (z-transform) to overcome problems with different dimensionality of the members of the set.
Calculation of the similarity between the objects of interest (squared Euclidean distance in the present study).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate squared Euclidean distance for similarity

steps.description: Choice of the mode of linkage between the objects (Ward's method of linkage) and graphical presentation of the linkage results in a tree-like diagram called a dendrogram.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Ward's method and create dendrogram

steps.description: In the final step, the statistical significance of the clusters is determined using Sneath's index (1/3 or 2/3 Dmax).
steps.searchable_summary: determine statistical significance using Sneath's index

steps.description: K-means non-hierarchical clustering is a supervised chemometric method where the number of clusters is a priori determined by a reasonable theoretical assumption (hypothesis) or expert opinion.
steps.searchable_summary: perform K-means clustering with predetermined number of clusters

steps.description: Principal components analysis (factor analysis) is a typical display method, which ensures the reduction in the size of the space of the variables.
The apportionment modeling uses principal components analysis as a first step of the data analysis followed by multiple regression on the principal components obtained in the first step.
steps.searchable_summary: perform apportionment modeling using PCA and regression

methods.description: This method describes the collection and analysis of sediment samples from the Varna gulf coastal line to assess pollution levels using various spectrometry techniques.

steps.description: 174 coastal sediment samples were collected during the drilling campaign in depth between 1 and 6 m for three conditional zones of the Varna gulf coastal line.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples from specified zones

steps.description: The samples were tested using gas chromatography coupled to mass spectrometry for polyaromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs) and polychlorinated biphenyl congeners (PCBs).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze samples using GC-MS for PAHs and PCBs

steps.description: Inductively coupled plasma-atomic absorption spectrometry (ICP-AAS) for metals as mobile and total forms (Cu, Zn, Ni, Cr), electrothermal AAS for Pb, and hybrid generation AAS for As.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze metals using ICP-AAS, electrothermal AAS, and hybrid generation AAS

steps.description: The total amount of phosphorus was determined as orthophosphate after treatment of the sample with perchloric acid and the application of the ascorbic acid reduction method.
steps.searchable_summary: determine phosphorus as orthophosphate using perchloric acid and ascorbic acid reduction

steps.description: Total nitrogen was determined by the Kjeldahl method.
steps.searchable_summary: determine total nitrogen using Kjeldahl method

steps.description: Humidity was measured by standard measurement of weight loss after drying and loss of ignition by weight loss after burning away the organic matter.
steps.searchable_summary: measure humidity and loss of ignition by weight loss methods
